ogTitle: Lake Brown vs Lake Camm: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lake Brown and Lake Camm.
heading: Lake Brown vs Lake Camm — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lake Brown, Western Australia and Lake Camm, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lake Camm has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Lake Brown (46/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lake Brown or Lake Camm safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lake Brown has a flood risk score of 58/100, while Lake Camm scores 40/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lake Brown scores 62/100 for bushfire risk. Lake Camm scores 28/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lake Brown or Lake Camm?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lake Camm has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.33% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
